overall_summary: The ElizaOS project is focused on foundational hardening and infrastructure modernization, including a transition to Clean Architecture and the launch of a new cross-platform secrets vault. Meanwhile, project leadership is managing significant community tension regarding token economics by emphasizing long-term product revenue over speculative price action.
key_facts: PR #7197 introduced @elizaos/vault, a cross-platform secrets management system integrating with OS keychains.
The ElizaOS team resolved process-level segfaults on headless Linux by implementing a D-Bus detection fallback for the keyring library.
PR #7235 performed a major repository restructuring, integrating cloud support while removing legacy Rust and Python components.
Shaw released three key repositories: plugin-auto-trader, plugin-social-alpha, and the Spartan project to the community.
Memory rot research by sentient_dawn identified a three-month degradation cycle in RAG architectures, leading to the implementation of freshness gates.
The Milady app is reported as nearly ready for launch to serve as a primary revenue generator for the ecosystem.
PR #7316 closed the n8n workflow clarification loop, allowing the host to request missing parameters from users during generation.
PR #7245 was merged to resolve a race condition between the Milady wrapper and the Telegram plugin that caused silent message loss.
open_questions: Does using backticks around URLs like `https://domain.com` work to bypass the spam filter?
What happened to the Babylon airdrop?

new_issues_prs.title: feat(vault): @elizaos/vault — cross-platform secrets vault + Settings UI integration
new_issues_prs.author: Dexploarer
new_issues_prs.significance: Crucial security update moving sensitive keys from plaintext config to encrypted OS vault.

new_issues_prs.title: chore: add cloud and plugins, remove rust and python
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Massive architectural cleanup and repository simplification.

new_issues_prs.title: refactor(cloud): Clean Architecture migration (WIP)
new_issues_prs.author: standujar
new_issues_prs.significance: Significant long-term refactor to resolve Redis socket/IO context issues on Cloudflare Workers.

overall_focus.claim: Development is centered on core stability, fixing authentication regressions (SIWE), and aligning the plugin ecosystem with ESM-first architecture.

discord_updates.summary: High-tension debate regarding ELIZAOS token price; Shaw emphasized that token holders are not traditional investors and revenue-generating products (Milady, Cloud) must precede buybacks.
Technical discussions regarding server spam filters blocking URLs; established a workaround using backticks. Research shared on memory rot in AI agents.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Community frustration over token price performance and perceived lack of clear communication regarding the roadmap and promised airdrops.

strategic_insights.insight: Leadership is actively pivoting the community away from speculative investment toward a 'community of builders,' signaling a longer 8-year maturity timeline analogous to traditional IPOs.
implications_or_questions: Will the current token holder base tolerate a long-term roadmap over short-term price support?
How will the launch of V3 and Eliza Cloud impact this sentiment?

strategic_insights.insight: Successful integration of Eliza into a Unitree robot demonstrates the framework's readiness for physical robotics applications beyond digital bots.
implications_or_questions: Should the project prioritize robotics-specific plugins given this success?

market_analysis.observation: ElizaOS is positioning itself as the 'Linux' of AI agents (open-source framework) compared to consumer-facing competitors like Orbofi.
market_analysis.relevance: Refines the target audience toward technical developers and enterprise-grade autonomous agent builders.
